description: Define ticket watch-list attribute contract in `tickets.attributes.watch_list` with normalized email and active state.
Implement watch-list parsing that tolerates null/legacy attribute shapes and produces normalized entries.
Deduplicate watch-list entries by normalized email during parse/merge operations.
Support watcher metadata fields (name/source/timestamps) without breaking minimum contract.
Add a Watch List section to ticket properties UI with recipient list visibility.
Add manual watcher email entry with validation and duplicate handling.
Add watcher active/inactive checkbox behavior in UI and persist it to ticket attributes.
Add watcher removal action in UI and persist deletion to ticket attributes.
Wire Watch List UI actions to existing ticket update action flow with permission enforcement.
Derive inbound watcher candidates from `To` and `CC` recipient lists during email ingestion.
Exclude sender and configured provider mailbox from inbound watcher candidate set.
Seed watch-list attributes on new ticket creation from inbound email recipients.
Upsert watch-list recipients on reply-to-existing-ticket inbound paths.
Reactivate inactive watcher entries when the same email appears again in inbound `To`/`CC`.
Read active watcher emails in ticket email subscriber from ticket attributes for outbound fan-out.
Send watcher notifications for ticket-created events using customer-visible context.
Send watcher notifications for ticket-updated events in both accumulator and non-accumulator paths.
Send watcher notifications for ticket-assigned events when customer-visible assignment notifications are emitted.
Send watcher notifications for ticket-comment-added events only when the comment is customer-visible.
Send watcher notifications for ticket-closed events with customer-visible context.
Deduplicate watcher recipients against existing recipients for each event send path.
Ensure comment-author suppression applies to watcher sends for public comment notifications.
Guarantee watcher delivery mode is separate individual emails (no outbound CC batching).
Allow any email, including technician/internal addresses, to be included as watchers via email-based list management.
Extend watch-list entry contract to support optional `entity_type` (`user|contact`) and `entity_id` metadata while preserving email-based canonical behavior.
Update watch-list parse/merge utilities to round-trip and merge entity metadata for duplicate emails without breaking existing legacy entries.
Add an internal-user picker add path in Watch List UI to create watcher entries from selected internal users.
Add a client-first contact picker add path in Watch List UI that defaults to contacts for the ticket's associated client.
Add a secondary `Search all contacts` path to select active cross-client contacts when the default client-scoped contact list is insufficient.
Prevent persistence when picker-selected user/contact has no valid email and surface a clear validation error.
Wire new watch-list picker data and handlers through TicketDetails -> TicketProperties -> TicketWatchListCard without regressing existing ticket properties behavior.
Add all-tenant active-contact lookup action(s) for watch-list fallback search using shared ticket-client contact query layer.
Keep manual email add path intact and fully interoperable with picker-added watchers in dedupe/reactivation flows.
Persist picker-added watcher metadata (`name`, `entity_type`, `entity_id`, `source=manual`) alongside normalized email and active state.
Display picker-added watcher identity hints (name and optional type badge) in the Watch List row UI while always showing recipient email.
Preserve outbound watcher send behavior as email-based fan-out; entity metadata must not alter send eligibility beyond existing active email logic.
Do not auto-disable watcher sends when linked user/contact becomes inactive; keep stored-email behavior unless user edits watch-list entry.
